scenarios.description: load() with systemProperties=false and empty .env list returns DotenvImpl wrapping only system environment entries
scenarios.precondition: Create an existing empty .env file in a temp directory; use real DotenvParser and DotenvReader
scenarios.expectedResult: returns a Dotenv instance whose entries() equals the current System.getenv() entries; no system properties set
pseudocode: GIVEN a temp directory with an empty .env file and DotenvBuilder.directory(path)
WHEN call builder.load() with systemProperties() not invoked
THEN assert result.entries() equals System.getenv().entrySet() and System.getProperty for each key is unaffected

scenarios.description: load() with systemProperties=true and zero entries applies no properties but returns DotenvImpl
scenarios.precondition: Create an empty .env file; call DotenvBuilder.systemProperties()
scenarios.expectedResult: returns a Dotenv instance whose entries() equals System.getenv(); no new System.getProperty values beyond existing ones
pseudocode: GIVEN a temp directory with empty .env and builder.systemProperties()
THEN assert result.entries() equals System.getenv().entrySet() and no additional system properties were set

scenarios.description: load() with systemProperties=false and one entry in .env returns DotenvImpl containing that entry plus system env
scenarios.precondition: Create .env file containing KEY=VALUE; use real parser and reader
scenarios.expectedResult: returns Dotenv where get("KEY") equals "VALUE" and entries() contains an entry for KEY=VALUE plus all System.getenv()
pseudocode: GIVEN a temp directory with .env containing "FOO=BAR"
THEN assert result.get("FOO") == "BAR" and entries() contains DotenvEntry("FOO","BAR")

scenarios.description: load() with systemProperties=true and multiple entries in .env sets each as system property and returns DotenvImpl
scenarios.precondition: Create .env file with multiple lines KEY1=V1 and KEY2=V2; call builder.systemProperties(); use real parser
pseudocode: GIVEN a temp directory with .env containing "A=1" and "B=2" and builder.systemProperties()
THEN assert result.get("A")=="1" && result.get("B")=="2" && System.getProperty("A")=="1" && System.getProperty("B")=="2"

scenarios.description: load() throws DotenvException when .env file is missing and throwIfMissing=true
scenarios.precondition: Point builder.directory to a non-existent path; use real DotenvReader and DotenvParser
scenarios.expectedResult: throws DotenvException; no system properties are modified
pseudocode: GIVEN builder with default throwIfMissing=true pointing to non-existent dir
THEN throws DotenvException and System.getProperty remains unchanged
